nodes.title: Consulting orgs
nodes.text: We consult organizations on how to build theories of change using the tool, then gather feedback on how to improve it.

connections.evidence: We have run two workshops demoing the beta tool and collected actionable feedback from participants. We already implemented many of their suggestions, which improved the tool's usability as reflected in higher user ratings post-modifications.
connections.assumptions: Consultants represent actual user needs; feedback is actionable; improvements are technically feasible within resource constraints

nodes.title: Development
nodes.text: Software development resources, technical expertise, and engineering capacity to build and maintain the tool.

connections.evidence: We have already completed the beta version of the tool and can use it to make ToCs (like this one).
connections.assumptions: Requirements are well-defined; technical architecture is sound; hosting/deployment infrastructure is reliable

connections.evidence: We've pivoted the tool significantly from the original vision based on feedback. Developers have already implemented most suggested features, and all project deadlines to date have been met, including the most difficult MVP deadline.
connections.assumptions: Development team prioritizes user experience; feedback mechanisms exist; technical debt doesn't accumulate

nodes.title: Outreach
nodes.text: Marketing, communication, and community engagement resources to reach potential users.

connections.evidence: Over 30 people from various organizations have already signed up to use the tool through targeted outreach and workshops at conferences.
connections.assumptions: Target audience is reachable through chosen channels; messaging resonates with EA strategy needs; timing aligns with planning cycles

nodes.title: Improvements to the tool
nodes.text: Continuous development and refinement to make the ToC builder tool as effective as possible.

connections.evidence: We have already improved the tool’s features and usability. AIM, the largest organization setting the standard for ToCs, has committed to using the improved tool.
connections.assumptions: Users can distinguish quality improvements; benefits outweigh switching costs; improvements address actual pain points not perceived ones

nodes.title: Tool actually exists
nodes.text: A functional Theory of Change builder tool is built, deployed, and accessible to users.

connections.evidence: We have had frequent follow-ups from organizations asking if the tool is ready to be used.
connections.assumptions: Tool is accessible, stable, and meets minimum usability standards; users have technical capability to access it

nodes.title: People reached who want tool
nodes.text: EA organizations and strategists who would benefit from ToC tools are made aware and engaged.

connections.evidence: We have already worked with two organizations to build their theories of change using the tool.
connections.assumptions: Target users actually want structured ToC processes; current planning methods are insufficient; adoption barriers are surmountable

nodes.title: Users build theories of change with the tool
nodes.text: EA organizations and individuals actively use the Theory of Change builder tool for strategic planning.

connections.evidence: The tool has already generated superior theories of change in under 30 minutes just by uploading internal documentation with minimal user input.
connections.assumptions: Tool design is intuitive enough to minimize learning curve; teams adopt structured processes; efficiency gains outweigh initial time investment

connections.evidence: The tool requires explicit assumption maps. Assuming organizations provide their ToC to their stakeholders or put it on their website, this will increase transparency.
connections.assumptions: Organizations actually share ToCs publicly or with stakeholders; tool generates formats suitable for external communication; transparency is valued over competitive advantage
Users engage deeply with causal reasoning rather than just filling templates; tool prompts improve rather than constrain thinking; teams have sufficient domain expertise

nodes.title: Users identify leverage points
nodes.text: Improved world models help EA orgs understand cause areas, intervention effects, and global priorities.

connections.assumptions: Organizations are willing to change cause focus based on new models; switching costs are manageable; funders support cause area pivots

connections.evidence: Using the tool has already helped some teams make more informed strategic decisions, though organizational inertia still affects outcomes.
connections.assumptions: Decision-makers use improved world models rather than relying on intuition; organizational processes incorporate new insights; analysis translates to strategy changes

nodes.title: Strategic planning takes less time
nodes.text: EA orgs achieve the same quality of strategic planning in less time using structured processes and tools, freeing capacity for high-impact activities.

connections.evidence: Teams have already saved time using the tool and reallocated it to research and planning activities.
connections.assumptions: Saved time is reallocated to high-value activities rather than absorbed by organizational slack; efficiency gains are sustained over time

nodes.title: Improved strategic plans
nodes.text: EA orgs make better strategic and operational decisions through enhanced processes and frameworks.

connections.assumptions: Organizations apply improved processes to research activities; decision-making improvements address the right bottlenecks; teams have sufficient domain expertise

nodes.title: Epistemic transparency/comms
nodes.text: EA orgs improve transparency in reasoning, evidence, and decision-making processes.

connections.assumptions: Funders actually reward transparency over other factors; transparent reasoning improves rather than hurts funding prospects; projects have sufficient underlying merit

nodes.title: Better interventions identified
nodes.text: Research identifies more cost-effective interventions within cause areas.

connections.assumptions: Organizations have capacity to adopt new interventions; new interventions fit organizational strengths and context; implementation quality matches research expectations

nodes.title: Funding applications approved
nodes.text: More funding applications to major EA funders are approved and deployed effectively.

connections.assumptions: Funding is deployed effectively rather than absorbed by overhead; resources are allocated to high-impact activities; organizational capacity scales with funding

nodes.title: EA orgs work on highest-impact causes
nodes.text: EA orgs focus resources on the most effective cause areas based on scale, neglectedness, and tractability.

connections.assumptions: Cause prioritization research identifies truly highest-impact areas; organizations can execute effectively in top cause areas; impact differences persist across contexts

nodes.title: EA orgs use most effective interventions
nodes.text: EA orgs select the most cost-effective interventions within their chosen cause areas.

connections.assumptions: Cost-effectiveness assessments accurately predict real-world impact; organizations can implement interventions as effectively as modeled; external validity holds across contexts

nodes.title: EA orgs that have strong ToCs get more resources
nodes.text: EA orgs gain access to funding, talent, and other resources needed to execute programs effectively.

connections.assumptions: Additional resources are deployed effectively; organizational systems can absorb increased capacity; scaling maintains intervention quality

nodes.title: EA orgs maximize their impact
nodes.text: Effective Altruism organizations optimize their impact by working on the right causes, using the best interventions, and securing adequate resources.

connections.evidence: EA organizations have already delivered measurable impact in global health, animal welfare, and x-risk, demonstrating that effective use of resources leads to reduced suffering.
connections.assumptions: EA organizations maintain evidence-based approach; impact measurement remains robust; cause area selection continues to be based on scale/neglectedness/tractability

nodes.title: Reduced suffering for humans and animals
nodes.text: Less suffering experienced by sentient beings worldwide.

title: Theory of Change Tool Theory of Change
